name: ICircuitBreaker
description: Contract for per-provider circuit breaker implementations. Tracks failure/success rates per provider and controls whether requests should be short-circuited to a fallback. The circuit transitions through three states: CLOSED → OPEN → HALF_OPEN → CLOSED.

methods.name: record_failure

params.name: provider
params.description: Identifier of the provider that failed

methods.description: Record a failed request for the given provider. Increments the failure counter. When the failure threshold is reached, the circuit transitions to OPEN.

methods.name: record_success

params.name: provider
params.description: Identifier of the provider that succeeded

methods.description: Record a successful request for the given provider. Resets the failure counter. In HALF_OPEN state, increments the success counter and transitions to CLOSED when the success threshold is reached.

methods.name: is_open

params.name: provider
params.description: Identifier of the provider to check

methods.description: Check whether the circuit is open for the given provider. Returns true when requests should be short-circuited (OPEN state within the recovery window). Returns false for CLOSED, HALF_OPEN, or OPEN-past-recovery-window.

methods.name: state

params.name: provider
params.description: Identifier of the provider to query

methods.description: Return the current circuit state for the given provider. Unknown providers return 'closed' (default state). Returns one of: 'closed', 'open', or 'half-open'.

behavioral_expectations: Must implement structural subtyping (Protocol) — no explicit inheritance required
Must be thread-safe — the circuit breaker may be shared across concurrent async tasks
All methods are synchronous — state transitions are in-memory operations
State transitions follow: CLOSED → OPEN (on failure threshold) → HALF_OPEN (after recovery window) → CLOSED (on success threshold)
Unknown/new providers must default to 'closed' state
record_success() in CLOSED state must reset the failure counter
record_success() in HALF_OPEN state must increment success counter toward CLOSED transition
is_open() must return false for HALF_OPEN state (allows probe requests)
state() must return one of exactly three values: 'closed', 'open', 'half-open'
